message:
fix(recording): wrap FrameWriter::drop flush in catch_unwind to avoid double-panic abort

Closes ft-7bcox: [LOW] FrameWriter Drop flush may abort on
double-panic during unwind (7c8deb1d smell). The bead was opened
during fresh-eyes review of 7c8deb1d, which added the drop-flush in
the first place.

The hole:
- FrameWriter::drop calls self.flush() to push any buffered frames
  out before the underlying writer is dropped.
- self.flush() can panic in practice — for example if the inner
  allocator OOMs while encoding a queued frame, or if the inner
  Write impl is a custom one (BufWriter wrap, network sink,
  pipe-backed file) that panics on bad state.
- Rust's panic-during-unwind rule aborts the process if a Drop
  panics while another panic is already unwinding the stack. The
  recording subsystem's Drop runs at process shutdown and during
  normal scope exit — exactly when an outer panic is most likely
  to be already in flight (operator panics, panicking workflow
  steps, OOM aborts elsewhere).
- Real-world risk is low because std File doesn't panic on I/O
  errors, but the hole is reachable via custom writers and OOM
  encode paths and is cheap to seal.

The fix:
- Wrap the drop-time flush call in
  std::panic::catch_unwind(AssertUnwindSafe(|| { let _ = self.flush(); })).
  Any panic raised by flush is now caught and discarded inside
  Drop instead of escaping into the unwinder. The outer panic (if
  any) propagates unchanged.
- The result of flush() is still ignored — Drop cannot fail and
  the existing comment already documents that Drop intentionally
  swallows flush errors. catch_unwind makes that swallow extend
  from "Result<(), _>" to "Result<(), _> *or* panic".

AssertUnwindSafe rationale (recorded inline in the doc comment):
- We discard the closure's Result, never observing &mut self
  again after the closure returns.
- The only state surviving the closure is self.buffer / self.writer,
  both of which are about to be dropped immediately afterwards by
  the regular field-drop sequence.
- Therefore neither the "panic leaves observer in a torn state"
  nor "panic invalidates an invariant a later method depends on"
  unwind-safety failure modes can apply here.

Scope:
- Surgical: only crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs's Drop
  impl is touched; flush() itself, the FrameWriter API, the
  buffer encoding path, and all callers are unchanged.
- No new tests: the abort path is unobservable from safe Rust
  without an injected panicking writer, and the existing
  recording tests already exercise the happy-path flush.

Co-author note: Patch authored by another agent in the same
multi-agent session; committed verbatim per the
"treat parallel-agent changes as your own" rule from AGENTS.md.
